operation_mapping.navigate_page: Use Codex browser or web tooling to open result, product, and supplier pages
operation_mapping.read_visible_content: Read only visible page content and mirror-visible metadata
operation_mapping.open_supplier_or_product_link: Traverse shortlisted supplier and product links in the same run
operation_mapping.write_markdown_report: Write or return a supplier-first Markdown report
operation_mapping.normalize_final_output: Persist the canonical Markdown report artifact; if the host output is already clean, keep the raw and canonical outputs identical

constraints: direct-site support assumes an operator-provided authenticated Chrome session; the operator may also need to clear the initial direct-search slider challenge before Codex takes over the live results page
automated login and self-managed session persistence are not validated for Codex
notes: Codex retains a validated mirror-assisted path, but its highest validated mode is now direct 1688 browsing under an operator-authenticated browser session.

A direct-site recheck on April 16, 2026 found that direct search still degraded into an anti-bot punish flow while direct product detail HTML was reachable outside the browser tool; browser-readable validated_direct support was not yet justified.

An operator-authenticated recheck on April 17, 2026 proved that Codex could read direct product-detail and supplier pages through a live authenticated Chrome session, but direct search remained intermittently punish-gated, so validated_direct was still not justified at that point.

A follow-up operator-cleared direct validation on April 17, 2026 started from a readable live search-results page, then completed search-result to product-detail to supplier-page traversal for a retained candidate and revisited two additional direct supplier/product pairs in the same session; validated_direct is now justified under operator_authenticated mode.
